title: DSDS Guideline
description: Documents actionable usage guidance for an artifact. Each item is a self-contained rule paired with a rationale explaining why. Guidelines tell the reader what to do (or not do) and why. They appear alongside other document block types in the unified document block array on any artifact.

guidelineEntry.description: A single, self-contained piece of usage guidance. Every guideline pairs an actionable statement with a rationale. `kind` classifies enforcement. `category` groups by discipline. `target` identifies which anatomy part or content area the guidance applies to. `criteria` is a polymorphic array — each entry is either an external reference (citing WCAG, platform guidelines, etc.) or an internal criterion definition (a stable, testable success criterion owned by this design system, citable via a tooling-managed display code).

guidance.description: The actionable guidance statement. MUST be concrete and unambiguous. Avoid subjective language like 'use sparingly' or 'when possible'.

rationale.description: Why this guidance exists. Cite evidence, accessibility standards, or user research when available. MUST NOT restate the guidance.

kind.description: The enforcement level, classifying how strictly the guideline should be followed. Values align with RFC 2119: 'required' (MUST — non-compliance is a defect), 'encouraged' (SHOULD — follow in most cases; exceptions need justification), 'informational' (MAY — advisory with no enforcement expectation), 'discouraged' (SHOULD NOT — avoid unless justified), 'prohibited' (MUST NOT — violations are defects). Defaults to 'informational'.

category.description: The discipline or domain this guideline belongs to. Used to group guidelines by concern. Standard values: 'visual-design' (color, typography, spacing, layout), 'interaction' (behavior, animation, transitions, gestures), 'accessibility' (inclusive design, assistive technology, WCAG compliance), 'content' (text, imagery, tone, voice, labeling), 'motion' (animation timing, easing, reduced-motion), 'development' (implementation patterns, performance, code conventions). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case.

target.description: The anatomy part or content area this guideline applies to (e.g., 'label', 'icon', 'helper-text'). When omitted, the guideline applies to the artifact as a whole.

criteria.description: Criteria addressed or defined by this guideline. Each entry is either an external reference (citing a published standard such as WCAG) or an internal criterion (defining a testable success condition owned by this design system). External references support citation of authoritative external rules. Internal criteria support WCAG-style governance: a stable, author-owned `identifier` anchors the criterion across edits, and tooling can derive citable display codes from those identifiers via a persistent registry. Authors SHOULD attach internal criteria only to guidelines whose success condition is objectively verifiable.

examples.description: Examples showing encouraged and discouraged approaches.

tags.description: Freeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories and disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation', 'contrast').

criterionReference.description: A reference to an external standard that this guideline addresses. Use for citing published standards (WCAG, MDN, platform docs, vendor compliance frameworks). Pair the URL with a descriptive label so the citation is meaningful in display.

kind.description: Identifies this entry as a reference to an external standard.

url.description: URL to the external standard or requirement (e.g., 'https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-minimum').

label.description: A human-readable label for the citation (e.g., '1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)', 'WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size'). When omitted, tools MAY derive a label from the URL.

criterionDefinition.description: A testable success criterion defined by this design system. Provides a stable, citable governance unit alongside the parent guideline's actionable guidance. Each definition pairs an author-owned `identifier` (which never changes once published) with a `title`, an objectively verifiable `successCriterion`, and optional techniques, failure conditions, examples, and external alignments. Tooling derives display codes (e.g., 'BTN-2.1') from the identifier via a persistent registry so that citations remain stable as guidelines are edited or reordered.

kind.description: Identifies this entry as an internal success criterion defined by this design system.

identifier.description: Stable, author-owned identifier scoped to the parent entity. MUST be lowercase kebab-case, MUST be unique within the parent entity, and MUST NOT change once published. Tooling SHOULD derive the display code (e.g., 'BTN-2.1', 'A11Y-1.4.3') from this identifier via a persistent registry; changing the identifier invalidates every citation that points at the criterion, so treat renames as deprecation + replacement.

title.description: A short human-readable title for the criterion (e.g., 'Touch Target Minimum', 'Single Primary Action'). Tools SHOULD render this alongside the display code when surfacing the criterion in docs or audit output.

successCriterion.description: The testable statement of what success looks like. MUST be objectively verifiable — either by inspection, static analysis, or runtime measurement. Avoid subjective language like 'feels right' or 'looks balanced'. Mirrors the role of a WCAG Success Criterion (e.g., 'The visual presentation of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1, except for large text, incidental text, and logotypes').

techniques.description: Ways to satisfy this criterion. Each entry is a richText description of a sufficient technique (e.g., 'Apply the `min-height: 44px` declaration to all interactive elements', 'Use the `<Button>` component without overriding its default height'). Maps to WCAG's 'Sufficient Techniques' list.

failures.description: Conditions that constitute a failure of this criterion. Each entry is a richText description of a known failure mode (e.g., 'Setting `height` below 44px on an interactive element', 'Removing `aria-label` from an icon-only button'). Maps to WCAG's 'Common Failures' list.

references.description: External standards this internal criterion aligns with or is derived from (e.g., a corresponding WCAG success criterion, an internal accessibility audit, a platform guideline). Each entry mirrors the shape of an external `criterionReference`.

url.description: URL to the aligned external standard.

label.description: A human-readable label for the aligned standard.

examples.description: Examples illustrating the criterion — typically before/after pairs showing passing and failing implementations.

tags.description: Freeform keywords to relate criteria across guidelines (e.g., 'a11y', 'contrast', 'touch-target', 'governance').

guideline.description: Documents actionable usage rules for an artifact. Each item pairs a guidance statement with a rationale. Categorize by discipline, target specific anatomy parts, and link to external standards like WCAG. Guidelines complement other document block types — use-cases tell the reader *when* to use the artifact, guidelines tell the reader *how* to use it correctly.

items.description: The guideline rules. Each item is a guidance statement paired with a rationale. Ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display guidelines in this order. Authors SHOULD order by importance or group by category.

kind.description: Identifies this as a guideline spec.

agents.description: Agent-optimized context for this document block. Provides structured information for AI/LLM consumption — constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords.
